# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.178.0/http/server.ts";

console.log("Web Scraping Service started.");

serve(async (req: Request) => {
  try {
    const url = new URL(req.url);
    const targetUrl = url.searchParams.get("url");

    if (!targetUrl) {
      return new Response("Please provide a 'url' query parameter.", { status: 400 });
    }

    // Request network access for the entire origin
    const status = await Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net" });

    if (status.state !== "granted") {
      return new Response("Network access permission denied.", { status: 403 });
    }

    // Fetch the content from the target URL
    const response = await fetch(targetUrl);

    if (!response.ok) {
      return new Response(`Failed to fetch content from ${targetUrl}: ${response.statusText}`, {
        status: response.status,
      });
    }

    const contentType = response.headers.get("Content-Type") || "text/plain";
    const content = await response.text(); // Or response.blob() / response.arrayBuffer() for binary data

    return new Response(content, {
      headers: { "Content-Type": contentType },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error processing request:", error);
    return new Response(`Internal Server Error: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
});
